tools.name: acc_list_projects
tools.description:
Enumerate every ACC and BIM 360 project the authenticated APS app can see by walking all accessible hubs and their project lists.

When to use: The agent needs to discover project IDs before calling any other tool (e.g. the user says 'show me my projects' or 'find issues in the Tower project' and no

tools.name: acc_create_issue
tools.description:
Create a new ACC issue (field observation, coordination clash, safety, quality, etc.) in the target project via the APS Construction Issues API.

When to use: The user wants to log a new issue — e.g. 'open a high-priority issue about the leaking valve on level 3' or a downstream agent detected a def

tools.name: acc_update_issue
tools.description:
Patch an existing ACC issue — change status, priority, assignee, or description via the APS Construction Issues API.

When to use: The user asks to close/reopen/escalate an issue, reassign it, or edit its body. Typical agent flow: acc_list_issues → pick an id → acc_update_issue.

When NOT to use: Do

tools.name: acc_list_issues
tools.description:
List and filter issues from a single ACC project (limit 50 per call) via the APS Construction Issues API.

When to use: The user or upstream agent needs to review open issues, count issues by status/priority, or look up an issue_id before calling acc_update_issue. E.g. 'show me all critical open iss

tools.name: acc_create_rfi
tools.description:
Create a new Request For Information (RFI) in an ACC project via the APS Construction RFIs API. RFI is created in 'draft' status — the project workflow owner typically transitions it to 'submitted'.

When to use: The user needs a formal question-of-record to the design or GC team — e.g. 'raise an RF

tools.name: acc_list_rfis
tools.description:
List and filter RFIs from a single ACC project (limit 50 per call) via the APS Construction RFIs API.

When to use: The user wants to review open RFIs, count outstanding ones, or look up an RFI ID. E.g. 'how many RFIs are still open on the Tower project?'

When NOT to use: Do not use for issues (use

tools.name: acc_search_documents
tools.description:
Full-text search the ACC Docs repository of a project for drawings, specs, submittals, and other files via the APS Data Management search endpoint.

When to use: The user wants to find a document by keyword (filename, sheet number, or metadata match). E.g. 'find the latest A-201 sheet' or 'search fo

tools.name: acc_upload_file
tools.description:
Upload a file from a public source URL into an ACC project folder. Runs the full four-step APS Data Management flow: top-folder discovery → storage object creation → OSS PUT of bytes → first-version item creation.

When to use: The user wants to push a document/photo/model into ACC Docs — e.g. 'uplo

tools.name: acc_project_summary
tools.description:
Fetch the full ACC project metadata record (name, type, status, dates, extension attributes) for a single project via APS Data Management. If hub_id is omitted the tool picks the first accessible hub, which may be wrong on multi-hub tenants.

When to use: The user asks 'tell me about project X' or a

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "acc_list_projects" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Enumerate every ACC and BIM 360 project the authenticated APS app can see by walking all accessible hubs and their project lists. When to use: The agent needs t…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "acc_list_issues" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: List and filter issues from a single ACC project (limit 50 per call) via the APS Construction Issues API. When to use: The user or upstream agent needs to revie…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: acc_list_projects, acc_create_issue, acc_update_issue, acc_list_issues, acc_create_rfi, acc_list_rfis, acc_search_documents, acc_upload_file · Private data: acc_list_projects, acc_create_issue, acc_update_issue, acc_list_issues, acc_create_rfi, acc_list_rfis, acc_search_documents, acc_upload_file, acc_project_summary · Public sink: acc_create_issue, acc_create_rfi, acc_search_documents, acc_upload_file · Destructive: acc_create_issue.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: acc_list_projects, acc_create_issue, acc_update_issue, acc_list_issues, acc_create_rfi, acc_list_rfis, acc_search_documents, acc_upload_file · Private data: acc_list_projects, acc_create_issue, acc_update_issue, acc_list_issues, acc_create_rfi, acc_list_rfis, acc_search_documents, acc_upload_file, acc_project_summary · Public sink: acc_create_issue, acc_create_rfi, acc_search_documents, acc_upload_file · Destructive: acc_create_issue

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
